title: RSF-SPLM-N Shelling Kills 14 Civilians Including 5 Children in Dilling; Sudan Doctors Warn of El Fasher-Scale Catastrophe
detail: After two days of assault, the RSF-SPLM-N offensive on Dilling, South Kordofan confirmed at least 14 civilians killed (including 5 children and 2 women) and 23 wounded as of March 29, per the Sudan Doctors Network. The shelling targeted residential areas in the city, causing 'a high number of casualties amid extremely difficult humanitarian and health conditions.' Eyewitnesses reported more than eight houses destroyed. The Sudan Doctors Network and local resistance leader Abdelrahman Hammad warned Dilling could face a 'catastrophic scenario' comparable to El Fasher in October 2025, where the RSF killed over 6,000 people in three days. The al-Tukma area (7 km east) remained under RSF-SPLM-N control, keeping the road to Habila severed and triggering a severe shortage of basic commodities in the city.

media.caption: RSF-SPLM-N shelling kills 14 civilians including 5 children in Dilling

title: US Pushes UNSC Humanitarian Ceasefire Resolution; UN Sanctions Hemedti's Brother and 3 RSF Commanders
detail: The United States, amid escalating violence, worked on a draft UN Security Council resolution demanding all parties immediately cease hostilities and facilitate humanitarian access to the most affected areas. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio held talks with Saudi, Egyptian, and UAE counterparts to coordinate the push; US Deputy Secretary of State Christopher Landau met Egyptian Foreign Minister Badr Abdelatty in Cairo. Separately, the UN Security Council imposed targeted sanctions on four RSF commanders for atrocities committed in El Fasher: Hemedti's brother Abdul Rahim Dagalo, Gedo Hamdan Ahmed, Elfateh Abdullah Idris Adam, and Tijani Ibrahim Moussa Mohamed. The Council also strongly condemned 'repeated drone attacks on civilians' and noted such attacks 'may constitute war crimes.' Analysts assessed ceasefire prospects as uncertain given SAF's refusal to negotiate while RSF holds any Sudanese territory.

media.caption: US pushes UNSC humanitarian ceasefire resolution for Sudan
